sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: BBB-, Moody's: Baa3, Fitch: BBB-

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: Oman's Duqm commercial port was struck by two Iranian drones, marking the first hostile attack on Omani infrastructure. Oman has 62% supply chain chokepoint exposure to the Strait of Hormuz. Oman's traditionally neutral diplomatic stance -- it has long served as a back-channel between Iran and the West -- is challenged by the direct military attack. GDP growth forecast at 2.6% is now under downward pressure.
key_changes_this_week: Two Iranian drones struck Oman's Duqm commercial port in Al Wusta Governorate (major)
Strait of Hormuz 70% traffic collapse threatens Oman's oil exports (62% chokepoint exposure)
Oman's historic neutral mediator role between Iran and the West under severe strain
narrative.outlook: Oman's economic diversification under Vision 2040 was progressing steadily, but the direct Iranian attack and Hormuz disruption create significant setbacks. Oman's Duqm port and free zone, designed as an alternative to Hormuz-dependent ports, ironically became a target. Tourism and logistics sectors face near-term headwinds. Oman's fiscal buffers are thinner than UAE or Qatar.
narrative.investor_implications: Oman sovereign debt (Ba1/BB) carries higher risk than Gulf peers. Fiscal position is more constrained with limited SWF buffer. Duqm free zone investments face elevated risk. However, if Duqm port can position as alternative to Hormuz-transiting routes, it could benefit from rerouting in the medium term.
narrative.data_quality_note: Oman macro data is current. Duqm port damage assessment is preliminary. Oman's relatively opaque fiscal reporting adds uncertainty to projections.

active_alerts.title: Oman critically exposed to Strait of Hormuz closure (62% chokepoint exposure)
active_alerts.description: The Strait of Hormuz experienced a 70% traffic collapse following US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Iran's IRGC broadcast VHF warnings closing the strait, attacked 3 commercial ships, and threatened all vessels. Oman has 62% supply chain chokepoint exposure. Oil and LNG exports are severely disrupted. This is the most severe global energy supply disruption in decades and directly threatens Oman's fiscal position and economic stability.

economy.sovereign_credit_rating: S&P: BBB-, Moody's: Baa3, Fitch: BBB-
